description: Service Scheduling and shop management — enforces estimate disclosure requirements, repair authorization limits, parts return obligations, and optimizes shop capacity and technician productivity

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any service scheduling operation
AI-generated scheduling recommendations are decision-support tools — not a substitute for service manager judgment
repair order data must be consistent — no mismatched labor times, parts, or pricing
state law requires a written estimate before beginning repairs — customer must receive and acknowledge the estimate
repair costs must not exceed the authorized amount by more than 10% without additional customer authorization — state automotive repair acts
replaced parts must be offered back to the customer upon request — state automotive repair act requirement
no work may begin without documented customer authorization for the specific repairs
open safety recalls must be disclosed to the customer when the vehicle is in for service

soft_flags.logic: scheduled appointments exceed available technician capacity for the time slot
a customer waiting on-site has exceeded their promised completion time
repair order has been open longer than the benchmark cycle time for its repair type
technician productivity (hours flagged / hours available) is below the configured target
shop utilization is significantly below capacity — opportunity to schedule additional work
